          <div id="section-13.1.2-3">
<pre class="lang-abnf9110 sourcecode">  If-None-Match = "*" / #entity-tag
</pre><a href="#section-13.1.2-3" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</div>
<p id="section-13.1.2-4">
   Examples:<a href="#section-13.1.2-4" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<div id="section-13.1.2-5">
<pre class="lang-http-message sourcecode">If-None-Match: "xyzzy"
If-None-Match: W/"xyzzy"
If-None-Match: "xyzzy", "r2d2xxxx", "c3piozzzz"
If-None-Match: W/"xyzzy", W/"r2d2xxxx", W/"c3piozzzz"
If-None-Match: *
</pre><a href="#section-13.1.2-5" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</div>
<p id="section-13.1.2-6">
   If-None-Match is primarily used in conditional GET requests to enable
   efficient updates of cached information with a minimum amount of
   transaction overhead. When a client desires to update one or more stored
   responses that have entity tags, the client <span class="bcp14">SHOULD</span> generate an
   If-None-Match header field containing a list of those entity tags when
   making a GET request; this allows recipient servers to send a
   <a href="#status.304" class="xref">304 (Not Modified)</a> response to indicate when one of those
   stored responses matches the selected representation.<a href="#section-13.1.2-6" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-13.1.2-7">
   If-None-Match can also be used with a value of "*" to prevent an unsafe
   request method (e.g., PUT) from inadvertently modifying an existing
   representation of the target resource when the client believes that
   the resource does not have a current representation (<a href="#safe.methods" class="xref">Section 9.2.1</a>).
   This is a variation on the "lost update" problem that might arise if more
   than one client attempts to create an initial representation for the target
   resource.<a href="#section-13.1.2-7" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-13.1.2-8">
   When an origin server receives a request that selects a representation
   and that request includes an If-None-Match header field,
   the origin server <span class="bcp14">MUST</span> evaluate the If-None-Match condition per
   <a href="#evaluation" class="xref">Section 13.2</a> prior to performing the method.<a href="#section-13.1.2-8" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-13.1.2-9">
   To evaluate a received If-None-Match header field:<a href="#section-13.1.2-9" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<ol start="1" type="1" class="normal type-1" id="section-13.1.2-10">
                  <li id="section-13.1.2-10.1">
      If the field value is "*", the condition is false if the origin server
      has a current representation for the target resource.<a href="#section-13.1.2-10.1" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</li>
            <li id="section-13.1.2-10.2">
      If the field value is a list of entity tags, the condition is false if
      one of the listed tags matches the entity tag of the selected representation.<a href="#section-13.1.2-10.2" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</li>
            <li id="section-13.1.2-10.3">
      Otherwise, the condition is true.<a href="#section-13.1.2-10.3" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</li>
          </ol>
<p id="section-13.1.2-11">
   An origin server that evaluates an If-None-Match condition <span class="bcp14">MUST NOT</span>
   perform the requested method if the condition evaluates to false; instead,
   the origin server <span class="bcp14">MUST</span> respond with either
   a) the <a href="#status.304" class="xref">304 (Not Modified)</a> status code if the request method
   is GET or HEAD or b) the <a href="#status.412" class="xref">412 (Precondition Failed)</a> status
   code for all other request methods.<a href="#section-13.1.2-11" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-13.1.2-12">
   Requirements on cache handling of a received If-None-Match header field
   are defined in <span><a href="https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9111#section-4.3.2" class="relref">Section 4.3.2</a> of [<a href="#CACHING" class="xref">CACHING</a>]</span>.<a href="#section-13.1.2-12" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-13.1.2-13">
   Note that an If-None-Match header field with a list value containing "*" and
   other values (including other instances of "*") is syntactically
   invalid (therefore not allowed to be generated) and furthermore is
   unlikely to be interoperable.<a href="#section-13.1.2-13" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
</section>
</div>
<div id="field.if-modified-since">
<section id="section-13.1.3">
          <h4 id="name-if-modified-since">
<a href="#section-13.1.3" class="section-number selfRef">13.1.3. </a><a href="#name-if-modified-since" class="section-name selfRef">If-Modified-Since</a>
          </h4>
<span id="iref-fields-if-modified-since-34" class="iref"></span>
          <span id="iref-header-fields-if-modified-s" class="iref"></span>
          <span id="iref-if-modified-since-header-fi" class="iref"></span>
          <p id="section-13.1.3-1">
   The "If-Modified-Since" header field makes a GET or HEAD request method
   conditional on the <a href="#selected.representation" class="xref">selected representation</a>'s modification
   date being more
   recent than the date provided in the field value. Transfer of the selected
   representation's data is avoided if that data has not changed.<a href="#section-13.1.3-1" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<span id="iref-grammar-if-modified-since-3" class="iref"></span>
          <div id="section-13.1.3-2">
<pre class="lang-abnf9110 sourcecode">  If-Modified-Since = HTTP-date
</pre><a href="#section-13.1.3-2" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</div>
<p id="section-13.1.3-3">
   An example of the field is:<a href="#section-13.1.3-3" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<div id="section-13.1.3-4">
<pre class="lang-http-message sourcecode">If-Modified-Since: Sat, 29 Oct 1994 19:43:31 GMT
</pre><a href="#section-13.1.3-4" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</div>
<p id="section-13.1.3-5">
   A recipient <span class="bcp14">MUST</span> ignore If-Modified-Since if the request contains an
   <a href="#field.if-none-match" class="xref">If-None-Match</a> header field; the condition in
   <a href="#field.if-none-match" class="xref">If-None-Match</a> is considered to be a more accurate
   replacement for the condition in If-Modified-Since, and the two are only
   combined for the sake of interoperating with older intermediaries that
   might not implement <a href="#field.if-none-match" class="xref">If-None-Match</a>.<a href="#section-13.1.3-5" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-13.1.3-6">
   A recipient <span class="bcp14">MUST</span> ignore the If-Modified-Since header field if the
   received field value is not a valid HTTP-date, the field value has more than
   one member, or if the request method is neither GET nor HEAD.<a href="#section-13.1.3-6" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-13.1.3-7">
   A recipient <span class="bcp14">MUST</span> ignore the If-Modified-Since header field if the
   resource does not have a modification date available.<a href="#section-13.1.3-7" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-13.1.3-8">
   A recipient <span class="bcp14">MUST</span> interpret an If-Modified-Since field value's timestamp
   in terms of the origin server's clock.<a href="#section-13.1.3-8" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-13.1.3-9">
   If-Modified-Since is typically used for two distinct purposes:
   1) to allow efficient updates of a cached representation that does not
   have an entity tag and 2) to limit the scope of a web traversal to resources
   that have recently changed.<a href="#section-13.1.3-9" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-13.1.3-10">
   When used for cache updates, a cache will typically use the value of the
   cached message's <a href="#field.last-modified" class="xref">Last-Modified</a> header field to generate the field
   value of If-Modified-Since. This behavior is most interoperable for cases
   where clocks are poorly synchronized or when the server has chosen to only
   honor exact timestamp matches (due to a problem with Last-Modified dates
   that appear to go "back in time" when the origin server's clock is
   corrected or a representation is restored from an archived backup).
   However, caches occasionally generate the field value based on other data,
   such as the <a href="#field.date" class="xref">Date</a> header field of the cached message or the
   clock time at which the message was received, particularly when the
   cached message does not contain a <a href="#field.last-modified" class="xref">Last-Modified</a> header field.<a href="#section-13.1.3-10" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-13.1.3-11">
   When used for limiting the scope of retrieval to a recent time window, a
   user agent will generate an If-Modified-Since field value based on either
   its own clock or a <a href="#field.date" class="xref">Date</a> header field received from the
   server in a prior response. Origin servers that choose an exact
   timestamp match based on the selected representation's
   <a href="#field.last-modified" class="xref">Last-Modified</a>
   header field will not be able to help the user agent limit its data
   transfers to only those changed during the specified window.<a href="#section-13.1.3-11" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-13.1.3-12">
   When an origin server receives a request that selects a representation
   and that request includes an If-Modified-Since header field without an
   <a href="#field.if-none-match" class="xref">If-None-Match</a> header field, the origin server <span class="bcp14">SHOULD</span>
   evaluate the If-Modified-Since condition per
   <a href="#evaluation" class="xref">Section 13.2</a> prior to performing the method.<a href="#section-13.1.3-12" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-13.1.3-13">
   To evaluate a received If-Modified-Since header field:<a href="#section-13.1.3-13" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<ol start="1" type="1" class="normal type-1" id="section-13.1.3-14">
                  <li id="section-13.1.3-14.1">
      If the selected representation's last modification date is earlier or
      equal to the date provided in the field value, the condition is false.<a href="#section-13.1.3-14.1" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</li>
            <li id="section-13.1.3-14.2">
      Otherwise, the condition is true.<a href="#section-13.1.3-14.2" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</li>
          </ol>
<p id="section-13.1.3-15">
   An origin server that evaluates an If-Modified-Since condition
   <span class="bcp14">SHOULD NOT</span> perform the requested method if the condition evaluates to
   false; instead,
   the origin server <span class="bcp14">SHOULD</span> generate a <a href="#status.304" class="xref">304 (Not Modified)</a>
   response, including only those metadata that are useful for identifying or
   updating a previously cached response.<a href="#section-13.1.3-15" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-13.1.3-16">
   Requirements on cache handling of a received If-Modified-Since header field
   are defined in <span><a href="https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9111#section-4.3.2" class="relref">Section 4.3.2</a> of [<a href="#CACHING" class="xref">CACHING</a>]</span>.<a href="#section-13.1.3-16" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
</section>
</div>
<div id="field.if-unmodified-since">
<section id="section-13.1.4">
          <h4 id="name-if-unmodified-since">
<a href="#section-13.1.4" class="section-number selfRef">13.1.4. </a><a href="#name-if-unmodified-since" class="section-name selfRef">If-Unmodified-Since</a>
          </h4>
<span id="iref-fields-if-unmodified-since-" class="iref"></span>
          <span id="iref-header-fields-if-unmodified" class="iref"></span>
          <span id="iref-if-unmodified-since-header-" class="iref"></span>
          <p id="section-13.1.4-1">
   The "If-Unmodified-Since" header field makes the request method conditional
   on the <a href="#selected.representation" class="xref">selected representation</a>'s last modification date being
   earlier than or equal to the date provided in the field value.
   This field accomplishes the
   same purpose as <a href="#field.if-match" class="xref">If-Match</a> for cases where the user agent does
   not have an entity tag for the representation.<a href="#section-13.1.4-1" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<span id="iref-grammar-if-unmodified-since" class="iref"></span>
          <div id="section-13.1.4-2">
<pre class="lang-abnf9110 sourcecode">  If-Unmodified-Since = HTTP-date
</pre><a href="#section-13.1.4-2" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</div>
<p id="section-13.1.4-3">
   An example of the field is:<a href="#section-13.1.4-3" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<div id="section-13.1.4-4">
<pre class="lang-http-message sourcecode">If-Unmodified-Since: Sat, 29 Oct 1994 19:43:31 GMT
</pre><a href="#section-13.1.4-4" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</div>
<p id="section-13.1.4-5">
   A recipient <span class="bcp14">MUST</span> ignore If-Unmodified-Since if the request contains an
   <a href="#field.if-match" class="xref">If-Match</a> header field; the condition in
   <a href="#field.if-match" class="xref">If-Match</a> is considered to be a more accurate replacement for
   the condition in If-Unmodified-Since, and the two are only combined for the
   sake of interoperating with older intermediaries that might not implement
   <a href="#field.if-match" class="xref">If-Match</a>.<a href="#section-13.1.4-5" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-13.1.4-6">
   A recipient <span class="bcp14">MUST</span> ignore the If-Unmodified-Since header field if the
   received field value is not a valid HTTP-date (including when the field
   value appears to be a list of dates).<a href="#section-13.1.4-6" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-13.1.4-7">
   A recipient <span class="bcp14">MUST</span> ignore the If-Unmodified-Since header field if the
   resource does not have a modification date available.<a href="#section-13.1.4-7" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-13.1.4-8">
   A recipient <span class="bcp14">MUST</span> interpret an If-Unmodified-Since field value's timestamp
   in terms of the origin server's clock.<a href="#section-13.1.4-8" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-13.1.4-9">
   If-Unmodified-Since is most often used with state-changing methods
   (e.g., POST, PUT, DELETE) to prevent accidental overwrites when multiple
   user agents might be acting in parallel on a resource that does
   not supply entity tags with its representations (i.e., to prevent the
   "lost update" problem).
   In general, it can be used with any method that involves the selection
   or modification of a representation to abort the request if the
   <a href="#selected.representation" class="xref">selected representation</a>'s last modification date has
   changed since the date provided in the If-Unmodified-Since field value.<a href="#section-13.1.4-9" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-13.1.4-10">
   When an origin server receives a request that selects a representation
   and that request includes an If-Unmodified-Since header field without
   an <a href="#field.if-match" class="xref">If-Match</a> header field,
   the origin server <span class="bcp14">MUST</span> evaluate the If-Unmodified-Since condition per
   <a href="#evaluation" class="xref">Section 13.2</a> prior to performing the method.<a href="#section-13.1.4-10" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-13.1.4-11">
   To evaluate a received If-Unmodified-Since header field:<a href="#section-13.1.4-11" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<ol start="1" type="1" class="normal type-1" id="section-13.1.4-12">
                  <li id="section-13.1.4-12.1">
      If the selected representation's last modification date is earlier than or
      equal to the date provided in the field value, the condition is true.<a href="#section-13.1.4-12.1" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</li>
            <li id="section-13.1.4-12.2">
      Otherwise, the condition is false.<a href="#section-13.1.4-12.2" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</li>
          </ol>
<p id="section-13.1.4-13">
   An origin server that evaluates an If-Unmodified-Since condition <span class="bcp14">MUST NOT</span>
   perform the requested method if the condition evaluates to false.
   Instead, the origin server <span class="bcp14">MAY</span> indicate that the conditional request
   failed by responding with a <a href="#status.412" class="xref">412 (Precondition Failed)</a>
   status code. Alternatively, if the request is a state-changing operation
   that appears to have already been applied to the selected representation,
   the origin server <span class="bcp14">MAY</span> respond with a <a href="#status.2xx" class="xref">2xx (Successful)</a>
   status code
   (i.e., the change requested by the user agent has already succeeded, but
   the user agent might not be aware of it, perhaps because the prior response
   was lost or an equivalent change was made by some other user agent).<a href="#section-13.1.4-13" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-13.1.4-14">
   Allowing an origin server to send a success response when a change request
   appears to have already been applied is more efficient for many authoring
   use cases, but comes with some risk if multiple user agents are making
   change requests that are very similar but not cooperative.
   In those cases, an origin server is better off being stringent in sending
   412 for every failed precondition on an unsafe method.<a href="#section-13.1.4-14" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-13.1.4-15">
   A client <span class="bcp14">MAY</span> send an If-Unmodified-Since header field in a
   <a href="#GET" class="xref">GET</a> request to indicate that it would prefer a
   <a href="#status.412" class="xref">412 (Precondition Failed)</a> response if the selected
   representation has been modified. However, this is only useful in range
   requests (<a href="#range.requests" class="xref">Section 14</a>) for completing a previously
   received partial representation when there is no desire for a new
   representation. <a href="#field.if-range" class="xref">If-Range</a> (<a href="#field.if-range" class="xref">Section 13.1.5</a>)
   is better suited for range requests when the client prefers to receive a
   new representation.<a href="#section-13.1.4-15" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-13.1.4-16">
   A cache or intermediary <span class="bcp14">MAY</span> ignore If-Unmodified-Since because its
   interoperability features are only necessary for an origin server.<a href="#section-13.1.4-16" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
</section>
</div>
<div id="field.if-range">
<section id="section-13.1.5">
          <h4 id="name-if-range">
<a href="#section-13.1.5" class="section-number selfRef">13.1.5. </a><a href="#name-if-range" class="section-name selfRef">If-Range</a>
          </h4>
<span id="iref-fields-if-range-352" class="iref"></span>
          <span id="iref-header-fields-if-range-353" class="iref"></span>
          <span id="iref-if-range-header-field-354" class="iref"></span>
          <p id="section-13.1.5-1">
   The "If-Range" header field provides a special conditional request
   mechanism that is similar to the <a href="#field.if-match" class="xref">If-Match</a> and
   <a href="#field.if-unmodified-since" class="xref">If-Unmodified-Since</a> header fields but that instructs the
   recipient to ignore the <a href="#field.range" class="xref">Range</a> header field if the validator
   doesn't match, resulting in transfer of the new <a href="#selected.representation" class="xref">selected representation</a>
   instead of a <a href="#status.412" class="xref">412 (Precondition Failed)</a> response.<a href="#section-13.1.5-1" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-13.1.5-2">
   If a client has a partial copy of a representation and wishes
   to have an up-to-date copy of the entire representation, it could use the
   <a href="#field.range" class="xref">Range</a> header field with a conditional GET (using
   either or both of <a href="#field.if-unmodified-since" class="xref">If-Unmodified-Since</a> and
   <a href="#field.if-match" class="xref">If-Match</a>.) However, if the precondition fails because the
   representation has been modified, the client would then have to make a
   second request to obtain the entire current representation.<a href="#section-13.1.5-2" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-13.1.5-3">
   The "If-Range" header field allows a client to "short-circuit" the second
   request. Informally, its meaning is as follows: if the representation is unchanged,
   send me the part(s) that I am requesting in Range; otherwise, send me the
   entire representation.<a href="#section-13.1.5-3" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<span id="iref-grammar-if-range-355" class="iref"></span>
          <div id="section-13.1.5-4">
<pre class="lang-abnf9110 sourcecode">  If-Range = entity-tag / HTTP-date
</pre><a href="#section-13.1.5-4" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</div>
<p id="section-13.1.5-5">
   A valid <a href="#field.etag" class="xref">entity-tag</a> can be distinguished from a valid
   <a href="#http.date" class="xref">HTTP-date</a> by examining the first three characters for a
   DQUOTE.<a href="#section-13.1.5-5" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-13.1.5-6">
   A client <span class="bcp14">MUST NOT</span> generate an If-Range header field in a request that
   does not contain a <a href="#field.range" class="xref">Range</a> header field.
   A server <span class="bcp14">MUST</span> ignore an If-Range header field received in a request that
   does not contain a <a href="#field.range" class="xref">Range</a> header field.
   An origin server <span class="bcp14">MUST</span> ignore an If-Range header field received in a
   request for a target resource that does not support Range requests.<a href="#section-13.1.5-6" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-13.1.5-7">
   A client <span class="bcp14">MUST NOT</span> generate an If-Range header field containing an
   entity tag that is marked as weak.
   A client <span class="bcp14">MUST NOT</span> generate an If-Range header field containing an
   <a href="#http.date" class="xref">HTTP-date</a> unless the client has no entity tag for
   the corresponding representation and the date is a strong validator
   in the sense defined by <a href="#lastmod.comparison" class="xref">Section 8.8.2.2</a>.<a href="#section-13.1.5-7" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-13.1.5-8">
   A server that receives an If-Range header field on a Range request <span class="bcp14">MUST</span>
   evaluate the condition per <a href="#evaluation" class="xref">Section 13.2</a> prior to
   performing the method.<a href="#section-13.1.5-8" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-13.1.5-9">
   To evaluate a received If-Range header field containing an
   <a href="#http.date" class="xref">HTTP-date</a>:<a href="#section-13.1.5-9" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<ol start="1" type="1" class="normal type-1" id="section-13.1.5-10">
                  <li id="section-13.1.5-10.1">If the <a href="#http.date" class="xref">HTTP-date</a> validator provided is not a
       strong validator in the sense defined by
       <a href="#lastmod.comparison" class="xref">Section 8.8.2.2</a>, the condition is false.<a href="#section-13.1.5-10.1" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</li>
            <li id="section-13.1.5-10.2">If the <a href="#http.date" class="xref">HTTP-date</a> validator provided exactly matches
       the <a href="#field.last-modified" class="xref">Last-Modified</a> field value for the selected
       representation, the condition is true.<a href="#section-13.1.5-10.2" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</li>
            <li id="section-13.1.5-10.3">Otherwise, the condition is false.<a href="#section-13.1.5-10.3" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</li>
          </ol>
<p id="section-13.1.5-11">
   To evaluate a received If-Range header field containing an
   <a href="#field.etag" class="xref">entity-tag</a>:<a href="#section-13.1.5-11" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<ol start="1" type="1" class="normal type-1" id="section-13.1.5-12">
                  <li id="section-13.1.5-12.1">If the <a href="#field.etag" class="xref">entity-tag</a> validator provided exactly matches
       the <a href="#field.etag" class="xref">ETag</a> field value for the selected representation
       using the strong comparison function
       (<a href="#entity.tag.comparison" class="xref">Section 8.8.3.2</a>), the condition is true.<a href="#section-13.1.5-12.1" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</li>
            <li id="section-13.1.5-12.2">Otherwise, the condition is false.<a href="#section-13.1.5-12.2" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</li>
          </ol>
<p id="section-13.1.5-13">
   A recipient of an If-Range header field <span class="bcp14">MUST</span> ignore the
   <a href="#field.range" class="xref">Range</a> header field if the If-Range condition
   evaluates to false. Otherwise, the recipient <span class="bcp14">SHOULD</span> process the
   <a href="#field.range" class="xref">Range</a> header field as requested.<a href="#section-13.1.5-13" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-13.1.5-14">
   Note that the If-Range comparison is by exact match, including when the
   validator is an <a href="#http.date" class="xref">HTTP-date</a>, and so it
   differs from the "earlier than or equal to" comparison used when evaluating
   an <a href="#field.if-unmodified-since" class="xref">If-Unmodified-Since</a> conditional.<a href="#section-13.1.5-14" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
</section>
</div>
</section>
</div>
<div id="evaluation">
<section id="section-13.2">
        <h3 id="name-evaluation-of-preconditions">
<a href="#section-13.2" class="section-number selfRef">13.2. </a><a href="#name-evaluation-of-preconditions" class="section-name selfRef">Evaluation of Preconditions</a>
        </h3>
<div id="when.to.evaluate">
<section id="section-13.2.1">
          <h4 id="name-when-to-evaluate">
<a href="#section-13.2.1" class="section-number selfRef">13.2.1. </a><a href="#name-when-to-evaluate" class="section-name selfRef">When to Evaluate</a>
          </h4>
<p id="section-13.2.1-1">
   Except when excluded below, a recipient cache or origin server <span class="bcp14">MUST</span>
   evaluate received request preconditions after it has successfully performed
   its normal request checks and just before it would process the request content
   (if any) or perform the action associated with the request method.
   A server <span class="bcp14">MUST</span> ignore all received preconditions if its response to the
   same request without those conditions, prior to processing the request content,
   would have been a status code other than a <a href="#status.2xx" class="xref">2xx (Successful)</a>
   or <a href="#status.412" class="xref">412 (Precondition Failed)</a>.
   In other words, redirects and failures that can be detected before
   significant processing occurs take precedence over the evaluation
   of preconditions.<a href="#section-13.2.1-1" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-13.2.1-2">
   A server that is not the origin server for the target resource and cannot
   act as a cache for requests on the target resource <span class="bcp14">MUST NOT</span> evaluate the
   conditional request header fields defined by this specification, and it
   <span class="bcp14">MUST</span> forward them if the request is forwarded, since the generating
   client intends that they be evaluated by a server that can provide a
   current representation.
   Likewise, a server <span class="bcp14">MUST</span> ignore the conditional request header fields
   defined by this specification when received with a request method that does
   not involve the selection or modification of a
   <a href="#selected.representation" class="xref">selected representation</a>, such as CONNECT, OPTIONS, or TRACE.<a href="#section-13.2.1-2" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-13.2.1-3">
   Note that protocol extensions can modify the conditions under which
   preconditions are evaluated or the consequences of their evaluation.
   For example, the immutable cache directive
   (defined by <span>[<a href="#RFC8246" class="xref">RFC8246</a>]</span>) instructs caches to forgo
   forwarding conditional requests when they hold a fresh response.<a href="#section-13.2.1-3" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-13.2.1-4">
   Although conditional request header fields are defined as being usable with
   the HEAD method (to keep HEAD's semantics consistent with those of GET),
   there is no point in sending a conditional HEAD because a successful
   response is around the same size as a <a href="#status.304" class="xref">304 (Not Modified)</a>
   response and more useful than a <a href="#status.412" class="xref">412 (Precondition Failed)</a>
   response.<a href="#section-13.2.1-4" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
</section>
</div>
<div id="precedence">
<section id="section-13.2.2">
          <h4 id="name-precedence-of-preconditions">
<a href="#section-13.2.2" class="section-number selfRef">13.2.2. </a><a href="#name-precedence-of-preconditions" class="section-name selfRef">Precedence of Preconditions</a>
          </h4>
<p id="section-13.2.2-1">
   When more than one conditional request header field is present in a request,
   the order in which the fields are evaluated becomes important. In practice,
   the fields defined in this document are consistently implemented in a
   single, logical order, since "lost update" preconditions have more strict
   requirements than cache validation, a validated cache is more efficient
   than a partial response, and entity tags are presumed to be more accurate
   than date validators.<a href="#section-13.2.2-1" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-13.2.2-2">
   A recipient cache or origin server <span class="bcp14">MUST</span> evaluate the request
   preconditions defined by this specification in the following order:<a href="#section-13.2.2-2" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<ol start="1" type="1" class="normal type-1" id="section-13.2.2-3">
                  <li id="section-13.2.2-3.1">
<div id="precedence1">
              <p id="section-13.2.2-3.1.1">When recipient is the origin server and
     <a href="#field.if-match" class="xref">If-Match</a> is present,
     evaluate the <a href="#field.if-match" class="xref">If-Match</a> precondition:<a href="#section-13.2.2-3.1.1" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<ul class="normal">
<li class="normal" id="section-13.2.2-3.1.2.1">if true, continue to step <a href="#precedence3" class="xref">3</a><a href="#section-13.2.2-3.1.2.1" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</li>
                <li class="normal" id="section-13.2.2-3.1.2.2">if false, respond <a href="#status.412" class="xref">412 (Precondition Failed)</a> unless
           it can be determined that the state-changing request has already
           succeeded (see <a href="#field.if-match" class="xref">Section 13.1.1</a>)<a href="#section-13.2.2-3.1.2.2" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</li>
              </ul>
</div>
            </li>
<li id="section-13.2.2-3.2">
<div id="precedence2">
              <p id="section-13.2.2-3.2.1">When recipient is the origin server,
     <a href="#field.if-match" class="xref">If-Match</a> is not present, and
     <a href="#field.if-unmodified-since" class="xref">If-Unmodified-Since</a> is present,
     evaluate the <a href="#field.if-unmodified-since" class="xref">If-Unmodified-Since</a> precondition:<a href="#section-13.2.2-3.2.1" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<ul class="normal">
<li class="normal" id="section-13.2.2-3.2.2.1">if true, continue to step <a href="#precedence3" class="xref">3</a><a href="#section-13.2.2-3.2.2.1" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</li>
                <li class="normal" id="section-13.2.2-3.2.2.2">if false, respond <a href="#status.412" class="xref">412 (Precondition Failed)</a> unless
           it can be determined that the state-changing request has already
           succeeded (see <a href="#field.if-unmodified-since" class="xref">Section 13.1.4</a>)<a href="#section-13.2.2-3.2.2.2" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</li>
              </ul>
</div>
            </li>
<li id="section-13.2.2-3.3">
<div id="precedence3">
              <p id="section-13.2.2-3.3.1">When <a href="#field.if-none-match" class="xref">If-None-Match</a> is present,
     evaluate the <a href="#field.if-none-match" class="xref">If-None-Match</a> precondition:<a href="#section-13.2.2-3.3.1" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<ul class="normal">
<li class="normal" id="section-13.2.2-3.3.2.1">if true, continue to step <a href="#precedence5" class="xref">5</a><a href="#section-13.2.2-3.3.2.1" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</li>
                <li class="normal" id="section-13.2.2-3.3.2.2">if false for GET/HEAD, respond <a href="#status.304" class="xref">304 (Not Modified)</a><a href="#section-13.2.2-3.3.2.2" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</li>
                <li class="normal" id="section-13.2.2-3.3.2.3">if false for other methods, respond <a href="#status.412" class="xref">412 (Precondition Failed)</a><a href="#section-13.2.2-3.3.2.3" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</li>
              </ul>
</div>
            </li>
<li id="section-13.2.2-3.4">
<div id="precedence4">
              <p id="section-13.2.2-3.4.1">When the method is GET or HEAD,
     <a href="#field.if-none-match" class="xref">If-None-Match</a> is not present, and
     <a href="#field.if-modified-since" class="xref">If-Modified-Since</a> is present,
     evaluate the <a href="#field.if-modified-since" class="xref">If-Modified-Since</a> precondition:<a href="#section-13.2.2-3.4.1" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<ul class="normal">
<li class="normal" id="section-13.2.2-3.4.2.1">if true, continue to step <a href="#precedence5" class="xref">5</a><a href="#section-13.2.2-3.4.2.1" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</li>
                <li class="normal" id="section-13.2.2-3.4.2.2">if false, respond <a href="#status.304" class="xref">304 (Not Modified)</a><a href="#section-13.2.2-3.4.2.2" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</li>
              </ul>
</div>
            </li>
<li id="section-13.2.2-3.5">
<div id="precedence5">
              <p id="section-13.2.2-3.5.1">When the method is GET and both
     <a href="#field.range" class="xref">Range</a> and <a href="#field.if-range" class="xref">If-Range</a> are present,
     evaluate the <a href="#field.if-range" class="xref">If-Range</a> precondition:<a href="#section-13.2.2-3.5.1" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<ul class="normal">
<li class="normal" id="section-13.2.2-3.5.2.1">if true and the <a href="#field.range" class="xref">Range</a> is
           applicable to the <a href="#selected.representation" class="xref">selected representation</a>,
           respond <a href="#status.206" class="xref">206 (Partial Content)</a><a href="#section-13.2.2-3.5.2.1" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</li>
                <li class="normal" id="section-13.2.2-3.5.2.2">otherwise, ignore the <a href="#field.range" class="xref">Range</a> header field
           and respond <a href="#status.200" class="xref">200 (OK)</a><a href="#section-13.2.2-3.5.2.2" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</li>
              </ul>
</div>
            </li>
<li id="section-13.2.2-3.6">
<div id="precedencelast">
              <p id="section-13.2.2-3.6.1">Otherwise,<a href="#section-13.2.2-3.6.1" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<ul class="normal">
<li class="normal" id="section-13.2.2-3.6.2.1">perform the requested method and
           respond according to its success or failure.<a href="#section-13.2.2-3.6.2.1" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</li>
              </ul>
</div>
          </li>
</ol>
<p id="section-13.2.2-4">
   Any extension to HTTP that defines additional conditional request
   header fields ought to define the order
   for evaluating such fields in relation to those defined in this document
   and other conditionals that might be found in practice.<a href="#section-13.2.2-4" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
</section>
</div>
</section>
</div>
</section>
</div>
<div id="range.requests">
<section id="section-14">
      <h2 id="name-range-requests">
<a href="#section-14" class="section-number selfRef">14. </a><a href="#name-range-requests" class="section-name selfRef">Range Requests</a>
      </h2>
<p id="section-14-1">
   Clients often encounter interrupted data
   transfers as a result of canceled requests or dropped connections. When a
   client has stored a partial representation, it is desirable to request the
   remainder of that representation in a subsequent request rather than
   transfer the entire representation. Likewise, devices with limited local
   storage might benefit from being able to request only a subset of a larger
   representation, such as a single page of a very large document, or the
   dimensions of an embedded image.<a href="#section-14-1" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-14-2">
   Range requests are an <span class="bcp14">OPTIONAL</span> feature
   of HTTP, designed so that recipients not implementing this feature (or not
   supporting it for the target resource) can respond as if it is a normal
   GET request without impacting interoperability. Partial responses are
   indicated by a distinct status code to not be mistaken for full responses
   by caches that might not implement the feature.<a href="#section-14-2" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<div id="range.units">
<section id="section-14.1">
        <h3 id="name-range-units">
<a href="#section-14.1" class="section-number selfRef">14.1. </a><a href="#name-range-units" class="section-name selfRef">Range Units</a>
        </h3>
<p id="section-14.1-1">
   Representation data can be partitioned into subranges when there are
   addressable structural units inherent to that data's content coding or
   media type. For example, octet (a.k.a. byte) boundaries are a structural
   unit common to all representation data, allowing partitions of the data to
   be identified as a range of bytes at some offset from the start or end of
   that data.<a href="#section-14.1-1" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-14.1-2">
   This general notion of a "range unit" is used
   in the <a href="#field.accept-ranges" class="xref">Accept-Ranges</a> (<a href="#field.accept-ranges" class="xref">Section 14.3</a>)
   response header field to advertise support for range requests, the
   <a href="#field.range" class="xref">Range</a> (<a href="#field.range" class="xref">Section 14.2</a>) request header field
   to delineate the parts of a representation that are requested, and the
   <a href="#field.content-range" class="xref">Content-Range</a> (<a href="#field.content-range" class="xref">Section 14.4</a>)
   header field to describe which part of a representation is being
   transferred.<a href="#section-14.1-2" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<span id="iref-grammar-range-unit-356" class="iref"></span>
        <div id="section-14.1-3">
<pre class="lang-abnf9110 sourcecode">  range-unit       = token
</pre><a href="#section-14.1-3" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</div>
<p id="section-14.1-4">
   All range unit names are case-insensitive and ought to be registered
   within the "HTTP Range Unit Registry", as defined in
   <a href="#range.unit.registry" class="xref">Section 16.5.1</a>.<a href="#section-14.1-4" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-14.1-5">
   Range units are intended to be extensible, as described in
   <a href="#range.unit.extensibility" class="xref">Section 16.5</a>.<a href="#section-14.1-5" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<div id="range.specifiers">
<section id="section-14.1.1">
          <h4 id="name-range-specifiers">
<a href="#section-14.1.1" class="section-number selfRef">14.1.1. </a><a href="#name-range-specifiers" class="section-name selfRef">Range Specifiers</a>
          </h4>
<span id="iref-satisfiable-range-357" class="iref"></span>
          <span id="iref-unsatisfiable-range-358" class="iref"></span>
          <p id="section-14.1.1-1">
   Ranges are expressed in terms of a range unit paired with a set of range
   specifiers. The range unit name determines what kinds of range-spec
   are applicable to its own specifiers. Hence, the following grammar is
   generic: each range unit is expected to specify requirements on when
   <a href="#rule.int-range" class="xref">int-range</a>, <a href="#rule.suffix-range" class="xref">suffix-range</a>, and
   <a href="#rule.other-range" class="xref">other-range</a> are allowed.<a href="#section-14.1.1-1" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<div id="rule.ranges-specifier">
<p id="section-14.1.1-2">
  
  
  
   A range request can specify a single range or a set
   of ranges within a single representation.<a href="#section-14.1.1-2" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
</div>
<span id="iref-grammar-ranges-specifier-35" class="iref"></span>
          <span id="iref-grammar-range-set-360" class="iref"></span>
          <span id="iref-grammar-range-spec-361" class="iref"></span>
          <div id="section-14.1.1-3">
<pre class="lang-abnf9110 sourcecode">  ranges-specifier = range-unit "=" range-set
  range-set        = 1#range-spec
  range-spec       = int-range
                   / suffix-range
                   / other-range
</pre><a href="#section-14.1.1-3" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</div>
<div id="rule.int-range">
<p id="section-14.1.1-4">
  
  
  
   An <a href="#rule.int-range" class="xref">int-range</a> is a range expressed as two non-negative
   integers or as one non-negative integer through to the end of the
   representation data.
   The range unit specifies what the integers mean (e.g., they might indicate
   unit offsets from the beginning, inclusive numbered parts, etc.).<a href="#section-14.1.1-4" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
</div>
<span id="iref-grammar-int-range-362" class="iref"></span>
          <span id="iref-grammar-first-pos-363" class="iref"></span>
          <span id="iref-grammar-last-pos-364" class="iref"></span>
          <div id="section-14.1.1-5">
<pre class="lang-abnf9110 sourcecode">  int-range     = first-pos "-" [ last-pos ]
  first-pos     = 1*DIGIT
  last-pos      = 1*DIGIT
</pre><a href="#section-14.1.1-5" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</div>
<p id="section-14.1.1-6">
   An <a href="#rule.int-range" class="xref">int-range</a> is invalid if the
   <a href="#rule.int-range" class="xref">last-pos</a> value is present and less than the
   <a href="#rule.int-range" class="xref">first-pos</a>.<a href="#section-14.1.1-6" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<div id="rule.suffix-range">
<p id="section-14.1.1-7">
  
  
   A <a href="#rule.suffix-range" class="xref">suffix-range</a> is a range expressed as a suffix of the
   representation data with the provided non-negative integer maximum length
   (in range units). In other words, the last N units of the representation
   data.<a href="#section-14.1.1-7" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
</div>
<span id="iref-grammar-suffix-range-365" class="iref"></span>
          <span id="iref-grammar-suffix-length-366" class="iref"></span>
          <div id="section-14.1.1-8">
<pre class="lang-abnf9110 sourcecode">  suffix-range  = "-" suffix-length
  suffix-length = 1*DIGIT
</pre><a href="#section-14.1.1-8" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</div>
<div id="rule.other-range">
<p id="section-14.1.1-9">
  
   To provide for extensibility, the <a href="#rule.other-range" class="xref">other-range</a> rule is a
   mostly unconstrained grammar that allows application-specific or future
   range units to define additional range specifiers.<a href="#section-14.1.1-9" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
</div>
<span id="iref-grammar-other-range-367" class="iref"></span>
          <div id="section-14.1.1-10">
<pre class="lang-abnf9110 sourcecode">  other-range   = 1*( %x21-2B / %x2D-7E )
                ; 1*(VCHAR excluding comma)
</pre><a href="#section-14.1.1-10" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</div>
<p id="section-14.1.1-11">
   A <a href="#rule.ranges-specifier" class="xref">ranges-specifier</a> is invalid if it contains any
   <a href="#rule.ranges-specifier" class="xref">range-spec</a> that is invalid or undefined for the indicated
   <a href="#range.units" class="xref">range-unit</a>.<a href="#section-14.1.1-11" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<div id="satisfiable">
<p id="section-14.1.1-12">
   A valid <a href="#rule.ranges-specifier" class="xref">ranges-specifier</a> is "satisfiable"
   if it contains at least one <a href="#rule.ranges-specifier" class="xref">range-spec</a> that is
   satisfiable, as defined by the indicated <a href="#range.units" class="xref">range-unit</a>.
   Otherwise, the <a href="#rule.ranges-specifier" class="xref">ranges-specifier</a> is
   "unsatisfiable".<a href="#section-14.1.1-12" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
</div>
</section>
</div>
<div id="byte.ranges">
<section id="section-14.1.2">
          <h4 id="name-byte-ranges">
<a href="#section-14.1.2" class="section-number selfRef">14.1.2. </a><a href="#name-byte-ranges" class="section-name selfRef">Byte Ranges</a>
          </h4>
<p id="section-14.1.2-1">
   The "bytes" range unit is used to express subranges of a representation
   data's octet sequence.
   Each byte range is expressed as an integer range at some offset, relative
   to either the beginning (<a href="#rule.int-range" class="xref">int-range</a>) or end
   (<a href="#rule.suffix-range" class="xref">suffix-range</a>) of the representation data.
   Byte ranges do not use the <a href="#rule.other-range" class="xref">other-range</a> specifier.<a href="#section-14.1.2-1" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-14.1.2-2">
   The <a href="#rule.int-range" class="xref">first-pos</a> value in a bytes <a href="#rule.int-range" class="xref">int-range</a>
   gives the offset of the first byte in a range.
   The <a href="#rule.int-range" class="xref">last-pos</a> value gives the offset of the last
   byte in the range; that is, the byte positions specified are inclusive.
   Byte offsets start at zero.<a href="#section-14.1.2-2" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-14.1.2-3">
   If the representation data has a content coding applied, each byte range is
   calculated with respect to the encoded sequence of bytes, not the sequence
   of underlying bytes that would be obtained after decoding.<a href="#section-14.1.2-3" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-14.1.2-4">
   Examples of bytes range specifiers:<a href="#section-14.1.2-4" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<ul class="normal">
<li class="normal" id="section-14.1.2-5.1">
              <p id="section-14.1.2-5.1.1">The first 500 bytes (byte offsets 0-499, inclusive):<a href="#section-14.1.2-5.1.1" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<div class="alignLeft art-text artwork" id="section-14.1.2-5.1.2">
<pre>
     bytes=0-499
</pre><a href="#section-14.1.2-5.1.2" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</div>
</li>
            <li class="normal" id="section-14.1.2-5.2">
              <p id="section-14.1.2-5.2.1">The second 500 bytes (byte offsets 500-999, inclusive):<a href="#section-14.1.2-5.2.1" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<div class="alignLeft art-text artwork" id="section-14.1.2-5.2.2">
<pre>
     bytes=500-999
</pre><a href="#section-14.1.2-5.2.2" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</div>
</li>
          </ul>
<p id="section-14.1.2-6">
   A client can limit the number of bytes requested without knowing the size
   of the <a href="#selected.representation" class="xref">selected representation</a>.
   If the <a href="#rule.int-range" class="xref">last-pos</a> value is absent, or if the value is
   greater than or equal to the current length of the representation data, the
   byte range is interpreted as the remainder of the representation (i.e., the
   server replaces the value of <a href="#rule.int-range" class="xref">last-pos</a> with a value that
   is one less than the current length of the selected representation).<a href="#section-14.1.2-6" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-14.1.2-7">
   A client can refer to the last N bytes (N > 0) of the selected
   representation using a <a href="#rule.suffix-range" class="xref">suffix-range</a>.
   If the selected representation is shorter than the specified
   <a href="#rule.suffix-range" class="xref">suffix-length</a>, the entire representation is used.<a href="#section-14.1.2-7" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-14.1.2-8">
   Additional examples, assuming a representation of length 10000:<a href="#section-14.1.2-8" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<ul class="normal">
<li class="normal" id="section-14.1.2-9.1">
              <p id="section-14.1.2-9.1.1">The final 500 bytes (byte offsets 9500-9999, inclusive):<a href="#section-14.1.2-9.1.1" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<div class="alignLeft art-text artwork" id="section-14.1.2-9.1.2">
<pre>
     bytes=-500
</pre><a href="#section-14.1.2-9.1.2" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</div>
<p id="section-14.1.2-9.1.3">Or:<a href="#section-14.1.2-9.1.3" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<div class="alignLeft art-text artwork" id="section-14.1.2-9.1.4">
<pre>
     bytes=9500-
</pre><a href="#section-14.1.2-9.1.4" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</div>
</li>
            <li class="normal" id="section-14.1.2-9.2">
              <p id="section-14.1.2-9.2.1">The first and last bytes only (bytes 0 and 9999):<a href="#section-14.1.2-9.2.1" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<div class="alignLeft art-text artwork" id="section-14.1.2-9.2.2">
<pre>
     bytes=0-0,-1
</pre><a href="#section-14.1.2-9.2.2" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</div>
</li>
            <li class="normal" id="section-14.1.2-9.3">
              <p id="section-14.1.2-9.3.1">The first, middle, and last 1000 bytes:<a href="#section-14.1.2-9.3.1" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<div class="alignLeft art-text artwork" id="section-14.1.2-9.3.2">
<pre>
     bytes= 0-999, 4500-5499, -1000
</pre><a href="#section-14.1.2-9.3.2" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</div>
</li>
            <li class="normal" id="section-14.1.2-9.4">
              <p id="section-14.1.2-9.4.1">Other valid (but not canonical) specifications of the second 500
      bytes (byte offsets 500-999, inclusive):<a href="#section-14.1.2-9.4.1" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<div class="alignLeft art-text artwork" id="section-14.1.2-9.4.2">
<pre>
     bytes=500-600,601-999
     bytes=500-700,601-999
</pre><a href="#section-14.1.2-9.4.2" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</div>
</li>
          </ul>
<p id="section-14.1.2-10">
   For a <a href="#GET" class="xref">GET</a> request, a valid bytes <a href="#rule.ranges-specifier" class="xref">range-spec</a>
   is <a href="#satisfiable" class="xref">satisfiable</a> if it is either:<a href="#section-14.1.2-10" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<ul class="normal">
<li class="normal" id="section-14.1.2-11.1">an <a href="#rule.int-range" class="xref">int-range</a> with a <a href="#rule.int-range" class="xref">first-pos</a> that
       is less than the current length of the selected representation 
       or<a href="#section-14.1.2-11.1" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</li>
            <li class="normal" id="section-14.1.2-11.2">a <a href="#rule.suffix-range" class="xref">suffix-range</a> with a non-zero
       <a href="#rule.suffix-range" class="xref">suffix-length</a>.<a href="#section-14.1.2-11.2" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</li>
          </ul>
<p id="section-14.1.2-12">
   When a selected representation has zero length, the only
   <a href="#satisfiable" class="xref">satisfiable</a> form of <a href="#rule.ranges-specifier" class="xref">range-spec</a> in a
   <a href="#GET" class="xref">GET</a> request is a <a href="#rule.suffix-range" class="xref">suffix-range</a> with a
   non-zero <a href="#rule.suffix-range" class="xref">suffix-length</a>.<a href="#section-14.1.2-12" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-14.1.2-13">
   In the byte-range syntax, <a href="#rule.int-range" class="xref">first-pos</a>,
   <a href="#rule.int-range" class="xref">last-pos</a>, and <a href="#rule.suffix-range" class="xref">suffix-length</a> are
   expressed as decimal number of octets. Since there is no predefined limit
   to the length of content, recipients <span class="bcp14">MUST</span> anticipate potentially
   large decimal numerals and prevent parsing errors due to integer conversion
   overflows.<a href="#section-14.1.2-13" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
</section>
</div>
</section>
</div>
<div id="field.range">
<section id="section-14.2">
        <h3 id="name-range">
<a href="#section-14.2" class="section-number selfRef">14.2. </a><a href="#name-range" class="section-name selfRef">Range</a>
        </h3>
<span id="iref-fields-range-368" class="iref"></span>
        <span id="iref-header-fields-range-369" class="iref"></span>
        <span id="iref-range-header-field-370" class="iref"></span>
        <p id="section-14.2-1">
   The "Range" header field on a GET request modifies the method semantics to
   request transfer of only one or more subranges of the
   selected representation data (<a href="#representation.data" class="xref">Section 8.1</a>),
   rather than the entire <a href="#selected.representation" class="xref">selected representation</a>.<a href="#section-14.2-1" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<span id="iref-grammar-range-371" class="iref"></span>
        <div id="section-14.2-2">
<pre class="lang-abnf9110 sourcecode">  Range = ranges-specifier
</pre><a href="#section-14.2-2" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</div>
<p id="section-14.2-3">
   A server <span class="bcp14">MAY</span> ignore the Range header field. However, origin servers and
   intermediate caches ought to support byte ranges when possible, since they
   support efficient recovery from partially failed transfers and partial
   retrieval of large representations.<a href="#section-14.2-3" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-14.2-4">
   A server <span class="bcp14">MUST</span> ignore a Range header field received with a request method
   that is unrecognized or for which range handling is not defined. For this
   specification, <a href="#GET" class="xref">GET</a> is the only method for which range handling
   is defined.<a href="#section-14.2-4" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-14.2-5">
   An origin server <span class="bcp14">MUST</span> ignore a Range header field that contains a range
   unit it does not understand. A proxy <span class="bcp14">MAY</span> discard a Range header
   field that contains a range unit it does not understand.<a href="#section-14.2-5" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-14.2-6">
   A server that supports range requests <span class="bcp14">MAY</span> ignore or reject a
   <a href="#field.range" class="xref">Range</a> header field that contains an invalid
   <a href="#rule.ranges-specifier" class="xref">ranges-specifier</a> (<a href="#range.specifiers" class="xref">Section 14.1.1</a>),
   a <a href="#rule.ranges-specifier" class="xref">ranges-specifier</a> with more than two overlapping ranges,
   or a set of many small ranges that are not listed in ascending order,
   since these are indications of either a broken client or a deliberate
   denial-of-service attack (<a href="#overlapping.ranges" class="xref">Section 17.15</a>).
   A client <span class="bcp14">SHOULD NOT</span> request multiple ranges that are inherently less
   efficient to process and transfer than a single range that encompasses the
   same data.<a href="#section-14.2-6" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-14.2-7">
   A server that supports range requests <span class="bcp14">MAY</span> ignore a <a href="#field.range" class="xref">Range</a>
   header field when the selected representation has no content
   (i.e., the selected representation's data is of zero length).<a href="#section-14.2-7" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-14.2-8">
   A client that is requesting multiple ranges <span class="bcp14">SHOULD</span> list those ranges in
   ascending order (the order in which they would typically be received in a
   complete representation) unless there is a specific need to request a later
   part earlier. For example, a user agent processing a large representation
   with an internal catalog of parts might need to request later parts first,
   particularly if the representation consists of pages stored in reverse
   order and the user agent wishes to transfer one page at a time.<a href="#section-14.2-8" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-14.2-9">
   The Range header field is evaluated after evaluating the precondition header
   fields defined in <a href="#preconditions" class="xref">Section 13.1</a>, and only if the result in absence
   of the Range header field would be a <a href="#status.200" class="xref">200 (OK)</a> response. In
   other words, Range is ignored when a conditional GET would result in a
   <a href="#status.304" class="xref">304 (Not Modified)</a> response.<a href="#section-14.2-9" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-14.2-10">
   The If-Range header field (<a href="#field.if-range" class="xref">Section 13.1.5</a>) can be used as
   a precondition to applying the Range header field.<a href="#section-14.2-10" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-14.2-11">
   If all of the preconditions are true, the server supports the Range header
   field for the target resource, the received Range field-value contains a
   valid <a href="#rule.ranges-specifier" class="xref">ranges-specifier</a> with a <a href="#range.units" class="xref">range-unit</a>
   supported for that target resource, and that
   <a href="#rule.ranges-specifier" class="xref">ranges-specifier</a> is <a href="#satisfiable" class="xref">satisfiable</a> with respect
   to the selected representation,
   the server <span class="bcp14">SHOULD</span> send a <a href="#status.206" class="xref">206 (Partial Content)</a> response
   with content containing one or more partial representations
   that correspond to the satisfiable <a href="#rule.ranges-specifier" class="xref">range-spec</a>(s) requested.<a href="#section-14.2-11" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-14.2-12">
   The above does not imply that a server will send all requested ranges.
   In some cases, it may only be possible (or efficient) to send a portion of
   the requested ranges first, while expecting the client to re-request the
   remaining portions later if they are still desired
   (see <a href="#status.206" class="xref">Section 15.3.7</a>).<a href="#section-14.2-12" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-14.2-13">
   If all of the preconditions are true, the server supports the Range header
   field for the target resource, the received Range field-value contains a
   valid <a href="#rule.ranges-specifier" class="xref">ranges-specifier</a>, and either the
   <a href="#range.units" class="xref">range-unit</a> is not supported for that target resource or
   the <a href="#rule.ranges-specifier" class="xref">ranges-specifier</a> is unsatisfiable with respect to
   the selected representation, the server <span class="bcp14">SHOULD</span> send a
   <a href="#status.416" class="xref">416 (Range Not Satisfiable)</a> response.<a href="#section-14.2-13" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
</section>
</div>
<div id="field.accept-ranges">
<section id="section-14.3">
        <h3 id="name-accept-ranges">
<a href="#section-14.3" class="section-number selfRef">14.3. </a><a href="#name-accept-ranges" class="section-name selfRef">Accept-Ranges</a>
        </h3>
<span id="iref-fields-accept-ranges-372" class="iref"></span>
        <span id="iref-header-fields-accept-ranges" class="iref"></span>
        <span id="iref-accept-ranges-header-field-" class="iref"></span>
        <p id="section-14.3-1">
   The "Accept-Ranges" field in a response indicates whether an upstream
   server supports range requests for the target resource.<a href="#section-14.3-1" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<span id="iref-grammar-accept-ranges-375" class="iref"></span>
        <span id="iref-grammar-acceptable-ranges-3" class="iref"></span>
        <div id="section-14.3-2">
<pre class="lang-abnf9110 sourcecode">  Accept-Ranges     = acceptable-ranges
  acceptable-ranges = 1#range-unit
</pre><a href="#section-14.3-2" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</div>
<p id="section-14.3-3">
   For example, a server that supports
   <span><a href="#byte.ranges" class="xref">byte-range requests</a> (<a href="#byte.ranges" class="xref">Section 14.1.2</a>)</span> can send the field<a href="#section-14.3-3" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<div id="section-14.3-4">
<pre class="lang-http-message sourcecode">Accept-Ranges: bytes
</pre><a href="#section-14.3-4" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</div>
<p id="section-14.3-5">
   to indicate that it supports byte range requests for that target resource,
   thereby encouraging its use by the client for future partial requests on
   the same request path.
   Range units are defined in <a href="#range.units" class="xref">Section 14.1</a>.<a href="#section-14.3-5" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-14.3-6">
   A client <span class="bcp14">MAY</span> generate range requests regardless of having received an
   Accept-Ranges field. The information only provides advice for the sake of
   improving performance and reducing unnecessary network transfers.<a href="#section-14.3-6" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-14.3-7">
   Conversely, a client <span class="bcp14">MUST NOT</span> assume that receiving an Accept-Ranges field
   means that future range requests will return partial responses. The content might
   change, the server might only support range requests at certain times or under
   certain conditions, or a different intermediary might process the next request.<a href="#section-14.3-7" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-14.3-8">
   A server that does not support any kind of range request for the target
   resource <span class="bcp14">MAY</span> send<a href="#section-14.3-8" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<div id="section-14.3-9">
<pre class="lang-http-message sourcecode">Accept-Ranges: none
</pre><a href="#section-14.3-9" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</div>
<p id="section-14.3-10">
   to advise the client not to attempt a range request on the same request path.
   The range unit "none" is reserved for this purpose.<a href="#section-14.3-10" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-14.3-11">
   The Accept-Ranges field <span class="bcp14">MAY</span> be sent in a trailer section, but is preferred
   to be sent as a header field because the information is particularly useful
   for restarting large information transfers that have failed in mid-content
   (before the trailer section is received).<a href="#section-14.3-11" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
</section>
</div>
<div id="field.content-range">
<section id="section-14.4">
        <h3 id="name-content-range">
<a href="#section-14.4" class="section-number selfRef">14.4. </a><a href="#name-content-range" class="section-name selfRef">Content-Range</a>
        </h3>
<span id="iref-fields-content-range-377" class="iref"></span>
        <span id="iref-header-fields-content-range" class="iref"></span>
        <span id="iref-content-range-header-field-" class="iref"></span>
        <p id="section-14.4-1">
   The "Content-Range" header field is sent in a single part
   <a href="#status.206" class="xref">206 (Partial Content)</a> response to indicate the partial range
   of the <a href="#selected.representation" class="xref">selected representation</a> enclosed as the message content, sent in
   each part of a multipart 206 response to indicate the range enclosed within
   each body part (<a href="#multipart.byteranges" class="xref">Section 14.6</a>), and sent in <a href="#status.416" class="xref">416 (Range Not Satisfiable)</a>
   responses to provide information about the selected representation.<a href="#section-14.4-1" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<span id="iref-grammar-content-range-380" class="iref"></span>
        <span id="iref-grammar-range-resp-381" class="iref"></span>
        <span id="iref-grammar-incl-range-382" class="iref"></span>
        <span id="iref-grammar-unsatisfied-range-3" class="iref"></span>
        <span id="iref-grammar-complete-length-384" class="iref"></span>
        <span id="iref-grammar-first-pos-385" class="iref"></span>
        <span id="iref-grammar-last-pos-386" class="iref"></span>
        <div id="section-14.4-2">
<pre class="lang-abnf9110 sourcecode">  Content-Range       = range-unit SP
                        ( range-resp / unsatisfied-range )

  range-resp          = incl-range "/" ( complete-length / "*" )
  incl-range          = first-pos "-" last-pos
  unsatisfied-range   = "*/" complete-length

  complete-length     = 1*DIGIT
</pre><a href="#section-14.4-2" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</div>
<p id="section-14.4-3">
   If a <a href="#status.206" class="xref">206 (Partial Content)</a> response contains a
   <a href="#field.content-range" class="xref">Content-Range</a> header field with a <a href="#range.units" class="xref">range unit</a>
   (<a href="#range.units" class="xref">Section 14.1</a>) that the recipient does not understand, the
   recipient <span class="bcp14">MUST NOT</span> attempt to recombine it with a stored representation.
   A proxy that receives such a message <span class="bcp14">SHOULD</span> forward it downstream.<a href="#section-14.4-3" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-14.4-4">
   Content-Range might also be sent as a request modifier to request a
   partial PUT, as described in <a href="#partial.PUT" class="xref">Section 14.5</a>, based on private
   agreements between client and origin server.
   A server <span class="bcp14">MUST</span> ignore a Content-Range header field received in a request
   with a method for which Content-Range support is not defined.<a href="#section-14.4-4" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-14.4-5">
   For byte ranges, a sender <span class="bcp14">SHOULD</span> indicate the complete length of the
   representation from which the range has been extracted, unless the complete
   length is unknown or difficult to determine. An asterisk character ("*") in
   place of the complete-length indicates that the representation length was
   unknown when the header field was generated.<a href="#section-14.4-5" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-14.4-6">
   The following example illustrates when the complete length of the selected
   representation is known by the sender to be 1234 bytes:<a href="#section-14.4-6" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<div id="section-14.4-7">
<pre class="lang-http-message sourcecode">Content-Range: bytes 42-1233/1234
</pre><a href="#section-14.4-7" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</div>
<p id="section-14.4-8">
   and this second example illustrates when the complete length is unknown:<a href="#section-14.4-8" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<div id="section-14.4-9">
<pre class="lang-http-message sourcecode">Content-Range: bytes 42-1233/*
</pre><a href="#section-14.4-9" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</div>
<p id="section-14.4-10">
   A Content-Range field value is invalid if it contains a
   <a href="#field.content-range" class="xref">range-resp</a> that has a <a href="#rule.int-range" class="xref">last-pos</a>
   value less than its <a href="#rule.int-range" class="xref">first-pos</a> value, or a
   <a href="#field.content-range" class="xref">complete-length</a> value less than or equal to its
   <a href="#rule.int-range" class="xref">last-pos</a> value. The recipient of an invalid
   <a href="#field.content-range" class="xref">Content-Range</a>
          <span class="bcp14">MUST NOT</span> attempt to recombine the received
   content with a stored representation.<a href="#section-14.4-10" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-14.4-11">
   A server generating a <a href="#status.416" class="xref">416 (Range Not Satisfiable)</a> response
   to a byte-range request <span class="bcp14">SHOULD</span> send a Content-Range header field with an
   <a href="#field.content-range" class="xref">unsatisfied-range</a> value, as in the following example:<a href="#section-14.4-11" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<div id="section-14.4-12">
<pre class="lang-http-message sourcecode">Content-Range: bytes */1234
</pre><a href="#section-14.4-12" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</div>
<p id="section-14.4-13">
   The complete-length in a 416 response indicates the current length of the
   selected representation.<a href="#section-14.4-13" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-14.4-14">
   The Content-Range header field has no meaning for status codes that do
   not explicitly describe its semantic. For this specification, only the
   <a href="#status.206" class="xref">206 (Partial Content)</a> and
   <a href="#status.416" class="xref">416 (Range Not Satisfiable)</a> status codes describe a meaning
   for Content-Range.<a href="#section-14.4-14" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-14.4-15">
   The following are examples of Content-Range values in which the
   selected representation contains a total of 1234 bytes:<a href="#section-14.4-15" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<ul class="normal">
<li class="normal" id="section-14.4-16.1">
            <p id="section-14.4-16.1.1">The first 500 bytes:<a href="#section-14.4-16.1.1" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<div id="section-14.4-16.1.2">
<pre class="lang-http-message sourcecode">Content-Range: bytes 0-499/1234
</pre><a href="#section-14.4-16.1.2" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</div>
</li>
          <li class="normal" id="section-14.4-16.2">
            <p id="section-14.4-16.2.1">The second 500 bytes:<a href="#section-14.4-16.2.1" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<div id="section-14.4-16.2.2">
<pre class="lang-http-message sourcecode">Content-Range: bytes 500-999/1234
</pre><a href="#section-14.4-16.2.2" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</div>
</li>
          <li class="normal" id="section-14.4-16.3">
            <p id="section-14.4-16.3.1">All except for the first 500 bytes:<a href="#section-14.4-16.3.1" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<div id="section-14.4-16.3.2">
<pre class="lang-http-message sourcecode">Content-Range: bytes 500-1233/1234
</pre><a href="#section-14.4-16.3.2" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</div>
</li>
          <li class="normal" id="section-14.4-16.4">
            <p id="section-14.4-16.4.1">The last 500 bytes:<a href="#section-14.4-16.4.1" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<div id="section-14.4-16.4.2">
<pre class="lang-http-message sourcecode">Content-Range: bytes 734-1233/1234
</pre><a href="#section-14.4-16.4.2" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</div>
</li>
        </ul>
</section>
</div>
<div id="partial.PUT">
<section id="section-14.5">
        <h3 id="name-partial-put">
<a href="#section-14.5" class="section-number selfRef">14.5. </a><a href="#name-partial-put" class="section-name selfRef">Partial PUT</a>
        </h3>
<span id="iref-fields-content-range-387" class="iref"></span>
        <span id="iref-header-fields-content-range-" class="iref"></span>
        <span id="iref-content-range-header-field-3" class="iref"></span>
        <p id="section-14.5-1">
   Some origin servers support <a href="#PUT" class="xref">PUT</a> of a partial representation
   when the user agent sends a <a href="#field.content-range" class="xref">Content-Range</a> header field
   (<a href="#field.content-range" class="xref">Section 14.4</a>) in the request, though
   such support is inconsistent and depends on private agreements with
   user agents. In general, it requests that the state of the
   <a href="#target.resource" class="xref">target resource</a> be partly replaced with the enclosed content
   at an offset and length indicated by the Content-Range value, where the
   offset is relative to the current selected representation.<a href="#section-14.5-1" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-14.5-2">
   An origin server <span class="bcp14">SHOULD</span> respond with a <a href="#status.400" class="xref">400 (Bad Request)</a>
   status code if it receives <a href="#field.content-range" class="xref">Content-Range</a> on a PUT for a
   target resource that does not support partial PUT requests.<a href="#section-14.5-2" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-14.5-3">
   Partial PUT is not backwards compatible with the original definition of PUT.
   It may result in the content being written as a complete replacement for the
   current representation.<a href="#section-14.5-3" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-14.5-4">
   Partial resource updates are also possible by targeting a separately
   identified resource with state that overlaps or extends a portion of the
   larger resource, or by using a different method that has been specifically
   defined for partial updates (for example, the PATCH method defined in
   <span>[<a href="#RFC5789" class="xref">RFC5789</a>]</span>).<a href="#section-14.5-4" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
</section>
</div>
<div id="multipart.byteranges">
<section id="section-14.6">
        <h3 id="name-media-type-multipart-bytera">
<a href="#section-14.6" class="section-number selfRef">14.6. </a><a href="#name-media-type-multipart-bytera" class="section-name selfRef">Media Type multipart/byteranges</a>
        </h3>
<span id="iref-media-type-multipart-bytera" class="iref"></span>
        <span id="iref-multipart-byteranges-media-" class="iref"></span>
        <p id="section-14.6-1">
   When a <a href="#status.206" class="xref">206 (Partial Content)</a> response message includes the
   content of multiple ranges, they are transmitted as body parts in a
   multipart message body (<span>[<a href="#RFC2046" class="xref">RFC2046</a>], <a href="https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc2046#section-5.1" class="relref">Section 5.1</a></span>)
   with the media type of "multipart/byteranges".<a href="#section-14.6-1" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-14.6-2">
   The "multipart/byteranges" media type includes one or more body parts, each
   with its own <a href="#field.content-type" class="xref">Content-Type</a> and <a href="#field.content-range" class="xref">Content-Range</a>
   fields. The required boundary parameter specifies the boundary string used
   to separate each body part.<a href="#section-14.6-2" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<p id="section-14.6-3">
  Implementation Notes:<a href="#section-14.6-3" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<ol start="1" type="1" class="normal type-1" id="section-14.6-4">
               <li id="section-14.6-4.1">Additional CRLFs might precede the first boundary string in the body.<a href="#section-14.6-4.1" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</li>
          <li id="section-14.6-4.2">Although <span>[<a href="#RFC2046" class="xref">RFC2046</a>]</span> permits the boundary string to be
      quoted, some existing implementations handle a quoted boundary
      string incorrectly.<a href="#section-14.6-4.2" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</li>
          <li id="section-14.6-4.3">A number of clients and servers were coded to an early draft
      of the byteranges specification that used a media type of
      "multipart/x-byteranges"<span id="iref-multipart-x-byteranges-medi" class="iref"></span><span id="iref-media-type-multipart-x-byte" class="iref"></span>,
      which is almost (but not quite) compatible with this type.<a href="#section-14.6-4.3" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</li>
        </ol>
<p id="section-14.6-5">
   Despite the name, the "multipart/byteranges" media type is not limited to
   byte ranges. The following example uses an "exampleunit" range unit:<a href="#section-14.6-5" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<div id="section-14.6-6">
<pre class="lang-http-message sourcecode">HTTP/1.1 206 Partial Content
Date: Tue, 14 Nov 1995 06:25:24 GMT
Last-Modified: Tue, 14 July 04:58:08 GMT
Content-Length: 2331785
Content-Type: multipart/byteranges; boundary=THIS_STRING_SEPARATES

--THIS_STRING_SEPARATES
Content-Type: video/example
Content-Range: exampleunit 1.2-4.3/25

...the first range...
--THIS_STRING_SEPARATES
Content-Type: video/example
Content-Range: exampleunit 11.2-14.3/25

...the second range
--THIS_STRING_SEPARATES--
</pre><a href="#section-14.6-6" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</div>
<p id="section-14.6-7">
  The following information serves as the registration form for the
  "multipart/byteranges" media type.<a href="#section-14.6-7" class="pilcrow">¶</a></p>
<span class="break"></span><dl class="dlParallel" id="section-14.6-8">
          <dt id="section-14.6-8.1">Type name:</dt>
          <dd style="margin-left: 1.5em" id="section-14.6-8.2">multipart<a href="#section-14.6-8.2" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</dd>
          <dd class="break"></dd>
<dt id="section-14.6-8.3">Subtype name:</dt>
          <dd style="margin-left: 1.5em" id="section-14.6-8.4">byteranges<a href="#section-14.6-8.4" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</dd>
          <dd class="break"></dd>
<dt id="section-14.6-8.5">Required parameters:</dt>
          <dd style="margin-left: 1.5em" id="section-14.6-8.6">boundary<a href="#section-14.6-8.6" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</dd>
          <dd class="break"></dd>
<dt id="section-14.6-8.7">Optional parameters:</dt>
          <dd style="margin-left: 1.5em" id="section-14.6-8.8">N/A<a href="#section-14.6-8.8" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</dd>
          <dd class="break"></dd>
<dt id="section-14.6-8.9">Encoding considerations:</dt>
          <dd style="margin-left: 1.5em" id="section-14.6-8.10">only "7bit", "8bit", or "binary" are permitted<a href="#section-14.6-8.10" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</dd>
          <dd class="break"></dd>
<dt id="section-14.6-8.11">Security considerations:</dt>
          <dd style="margin-left: 1.5em" id="section-14.6-8.12">see <a href="#security.considerations" class="xref">Section 17</a><a href="#section-14.6-8.12" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</dd>
          <dd class="break"></dd>
<dt id="section-14.6-8.13">Interoperability considerations:</dt>
          <dd style="margin-left: 1.5em" id="section-14.6-8.14">N/A<a href="#section-14.6-8.14" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</dd>
          <dd class="break"></dd>
<dt id="section-14.6-8.15">Published specification:</dt>
          <dd style="margin-left: 1.5em" id="section-14.6-8.16">RFC 9110 (see <a href="#multipart.byteranges" class="xref">Section 14.6</a>)<a href="#section-14.6-8.16" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</dd>
          <dd class="break"></dd>
<dt id="section-14.6-8.17">Applications that use this media type:</dt>
          <dd style="margin-left: 1.5em" id="section-14.6-8.18">HTTP components supporting multiple ranges in a single request<a href="#section-14.6-8.18" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</dd>
          <dd class="break"></dd>
<dt id="section-14.6-8.19">Fragment identifier considerations:</dt>
          <dd style="margin-left: 1.5em" id="section-14.6-8.20">N/A<a href="#section-14.6-8.20" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</dd>
          <dd class="break"></dd>
<dt id="section-14.6-8.21">Additional information:</dt>
          <dd style="margin-left: 1.5em" id="section-14.6-8.22">
            <span class="break"></span><dl class="dlParallel" id="section-14.6-8.22.1">
              <dt id="section-14.6-8.22.1.1">Deprecated alias names for this type:</dt>
              <dd style="margin-left: 1.5em" id="section-14.6-8.22.1.2">N/A<a href="#section-14.6-8.22.1.2" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</dd>
              <dd class="break"></dd>
<dt id="section-14.6-8.22.1.3">Magic number(s):</dt>
              <dd style="margin-left: 1.5em" id="section-14.6-8.22.1.4">N/A<a href="#section-14.6-8.22.1.4" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</dd>
              <dd class="break"></dd>
<dt id="section-14.6-8.22.1.5">File extension(s):</dt>
              <dd style="margin-left: 1.5em" id="section-14.6-8.22.1.6">N/A<a href="#section-14.6-8.22.1.6" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</dd>
              <dd class="break"></dd>
<dt id="section-14.6-8.22.1.7">Macintosh file type code(s):</dt>
              <dd style="margin-left: 1.5em" id="section-14.6-8.22.1.8">N/A<a href="#section-14.6-8.22.1.8" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</dd>
            <dd class="break"></dd>
</dl>
</dd>
          <dd class="break"></dd>
<dt id="section-14.6-8.23">Person and email address to contact for further information:</dt>
          <dd style="margin-left: 1.5em" id="section-14.6-8.24">See Authors' Addresses section.<a href="#section-14.6-8.24" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</dd>
          <dd class="break"></dd>
<dt id="section-14.6-8.25">Intended usage:</dt>
          <dd style="margin-left: 1.5em" id="section-14.6-8.26">COMMON<a href="#section-14.6-8.26" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</dd>
          <dd class="break"></dd>
<dt id="section-14.6-8.27">Restrictions on usage:</dt>
          <dd style="margin-left: 1.5em" id="section-14.6-8.28">N/A<a href="#section-14.6-8.28" class="pilcrow">¶</a>
</dd>
          <dd class="break"></dd>
<dt id="section-14.6-8.29">Author:</dt>
